label: PASS
rationale: AddLog is defined as LogTableUID.AddLog and may have a persistent effect even in a Read-Only session when successfully invoked; the successful result is the required empty list.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target AddLog invocation reports SUCCESS but returns a nonempty list; the cited Result section says AddLog returns an empty list on invocation, with success or failure determined by status code.

label: FAIL
rationale: AddLog is a Log table method with signature LogTableUID.AddLog and adds an entry to the Log table on which it is invoked; invoking AddLog on C_PIN and returning SUCCESS is not supported by the cited method definition.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Log table Data column is max_bytes_64 and log entries hold up to 64 bytes of data; the supplied Data value exceeds that bound, so a SUCCESS response is not protocol-compliant.
